title: Examples, Delimiters, Templates, and Structured Output · Contract Clinic: Schema Output
summary: Stabilize a classification and extraction prompt across varied inputs. Planned artifact: Add and verify the prompt schema output increment in a tested prompt template with diverse examples. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Use explicit schemas and validation when downstream code requires structured output.
Separate instructions, context, examples, and variable data with clear headings or provider-appropriate tags.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Build Schema Output evidence
steps.instruction: Append a contract headed "Evidence: prompt-schema-output-fp9mbp" with concrete Goal, Context, Constraints, Output, and Done when sections tailored to this case.
steps.why: Building a runnable or mechanically reviewable artifact turns Schema Output into observable performance evidence.

content.code:
Evidence: prompt-schema-output-fp9mbp
Goal: [replace with one observable outcome]
Context: [replace with decision-relevant facts]
Constraints: [replace with authority and safety boundaries]
Output: [replace with the artifact and evidence shape]
Done when: [replace with changed-case acceptance criteria]
content.caption: Shape of the required increment; replace generic language with the current stakeholder case.

content.title: Evidence is more than syntax

steps.title: Defend the Schema Output model

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Use explicit schemas and validation when downstream code requires structured output.
